context: Tests whether the agent applies the slide source hierarchy (pptx > pdf > video_extracted > none), treats video_url as the processability gate, handles fallback chains, and sets correct status per the error handling matrix.

checklist.name: PPTX highest priority
checklist.description: When both PPTX and PDF are available (Microservices Myths), slide_source reflects that both are available — not just PDF

checklist.name: Video-only talks are processable
checklist.description: CI Pipeline Masterclass (video_url but no slides_url, no pptx_path) is NOT skipped — it gets slide_source indicating video extraction and can reach a processed status

checklist.name: No-video talks not processable
checklist.description: Testing in Production (has slides_url but no video_url) is marked as not processable / skipped — video_url is required to enter the processing pipeline

checklist.name: Multiple slide_source values used
checklist.description: The output database uses at least 3 distinct slide_source values across the 8 talks — reflecting the different acquisition paths

checklist.name: Video extraction output named by YouTube ID
checklist.description: For video-extracted talks, the output path or filename references the YouTube video ID (e.g., 'ghi789.pdf'), not a generic name

checklist.name: Slides fail + video available = fallback to video
checklist.description: DevOps Culture Reset (slides download failed but has video_url) attempts video extraction as fallback — the processing log shows the fallback chain

checklist.name: Partial processing when one source fails
checklist.description: Talks where transcript or slides (but not both) fail get a status distinct from full success — indicating partial analysis is possible

checklist.name: All fail = distinct failure status
checklist.description: GitOps Journey (both transcript and slides failed) gets a status indicating download failure, distinct from the missing-sources skip

checklist.name: Processing log explains decisions
checklist.description: The log explains the source selection rationale for at least 5 talks — not just listing outcomes but explaining why each source was chosen
